title: Signature replay across paths: path not in scope, only reconstructed locally
description:
server.rs:464-468: The path used in signature verification is reconstructed server-side from the URL route parameter, not taken from any header the client provides.

This means a valid Signature for POST /users/alice/inbox can be replayed to POST /inbox (shared inbox) because:
- The signed (request-target) is `post /users/alice/inbox` 
- When replayed to /inbox, server.rs reconstructs path as `/inbox` (line 466), so the verification string differs and the replay is caught... but only if (request-target) is in the signed headers list.

If the attacker omits (request-target) from their headers= list, the path is never included in the signed string, and the same signature validates against any path. Combined with broadside-0e74.3 (Digest not enforced), an attacker can replay any valid signed request to any inbox endpoint.

Additionally: there is no date/freshness window check. A signature from 6 months ago with no (request-target) and no digest is accepted indefinitely as long as the actor's key is still cached.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
